EF-0 TORNADO CONFIRMED WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 80 MPH IN LYNN TOWNSHIP. PATH LENGTH WAS 3 MILES AND MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 150 YARDS. NO INJURIES REPORTED. PROPERTY DAMAGE REPORTED, INCLUDING DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOUSES DUE TO MANY FALLEN TREES. ON THE LYNN ACRES FARM, THE ROOF OF THE BARN WAS PEELED BACK. MOST OF THE DAMAGE OCCURRED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF SECHLER ROAD AND WEDGE DRIVE.
STREET FLOODING ALONG MAIN AND PENNSYLVANIA STREETS IN TRAINER. CARS STUCK IN THE WATER ESTIMATED TO BE 3 FEET DEEP.

*** 1 INJ *** EVACUATIONS DUE TO FLASH FLOODING IN PRICES CORNER, INCLUDING A FEW BUSINESSES AND A DAYCARE. THREE FEET OF WATER FLOODED KIRKWOOD HIGHWAY BETWEEN MAPLE AVE AND SAUNDERS ROAD. FOURTEEN CARS PARKED AT THE VETERANS HOSPITAL WERE DAMAGED WHEN SUBMERGED UNDER NEARLY 4 FEET OF WATER. ONE MAN IN HIS 50S ASSISTING A VAN OUT OF THE WATER WAS INJURED WHEN THE VAN ROLLED OVER HIM. TIME APPROXIMATE BETWEEN 220PM AND 3PM.

FLOODING REPORTED ON MANY SIDE STREETS AND ROADWAYS NEAR THE BAY. KDOX DOPPLER RADAR STORM TOTAL ESTIMATES 4 INCHES NEAR VENTNOR CITY. SKULL BAY WATERSHED RAINFALL ESTIMATE 3.73 JUST WEST OF ATLANTIC CITY.

EF-0 TORNADO CONFIRMED WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 75 MPH IN THE CITY OF CHESTER. PATH LENGTH WAS 0.30 MILES AND MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 80 YARDS. NO INJURIES REPORTED. NO FATALITIES REPORTED. NUMEROUS TREES WERE FALLEN ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO. THERE WAS ALSO ROOF AND SIDING DAMAGE TO THREE HOUSES. MOST OF THIS DAMAGE OCCURRED IN THE VICINITY OF THIRD STREET JUST WEST OF THE COMMODORE BARRY BRIDGE. THERE WAS ALSO SOME DAMAGE TO THE NEW I-95 OFF RAMP UNDER CONSTRUCTION NEAR THE BRIDGE.
WIRES DOWN IN MANTUA, MONROE, PITMAN AND WOODBURY TOWNSHIPS. SOME LOCALIZED STREET FLOODING WAS ALSO REPORTED IN PITMAN.
